introSection:
Griffon is a steel roller coaster located at Busch Gardens Williamsburg in Williamsburg, Virginia, USA. It is themed after a half-lion/half-eagle mythological creature called a Griffon, known for diving on unsuspecting prey.

Opened in 2007, the ride was built by Bolliger & Mabillard and was the tallest and fastest Dive Coaster until it was surpassed by Valravn at Cedar Point in 2016. It is also the first to have multiple inversions and floorless trains.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On June 30, 2006, Busch Gardens Williamsburg announced that Le Mans Raceway would be closing down to make room for a new attraction set to open in 2007. The attraction officially closed on July 5.

On August 23, 2006, it was announced that Griffon would be coming to Busch Gardens Williamsburg. The highest point of the ride was topped off in December 2006.

Griffon opened on May 18, 2007.

In 2017, Griffon was repainted with a darker shade of blue in some areas.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Griffon has two vertical drops, two immelmanns and a splash effect. The first big drop dives under a pedestrian bridge. The second immelmann loops over the Rhine River near Loch Ness Monster.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Griffon has a blue track and white supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: A car from Griffon was on display at B&M's booth at the 2007 IAAPA Expo.

sections.title: In video games
sections.content: In Frontier Development's theme park simulation game Planet Coaster, the B&M Dive Coaster model (Aethon) has a color scheme matching Griffon's. The trains in the game are also ten across.

fullText:
Griffon is a steel roller coaster located at Busch Gardens Williamsburg in Williamsburg, Virginia, USA. It is themed after a half-lion/half-eagle mythological creature called a Griffon, known for diving on unsuspecting prey.

Opened in 2007, the ride was built by Bolliger & Mabillard and was the tallest and fastest Dive Coaster until it was surpassed by Valravn at Cedar Point in 2016. It is also the first to have multiple inversions and floorless trains.

On June 30, 2006, Busch Gardens Williamsburg announced that Le Mans Raceway would be closing down to make room for a new attraction set to open in 2007. The attraction officially closed on July 5.

On August 23, 2006, it was announced that Griffon would be coming to Busch Gardens Williamsburg. The highest point of the ride was topped off in December 2006.

Griffon opened on May 18, 2007.

In 2017, Griffon was repainted with a darker shade of blue in some areas.

Griffon has two vertical drops, two immelmanns and a splash effect. The first big drop dives under a pedestrian bridge. The second immelmann loops over the Rhine River near Loch Ness Monster.

Griffon has a blue track and white supports.

A car from Griffon was on display at B&M's booth at the 2007 IAAPA Expo.

In Frontier Development's theme park simulation game Planet Coaster, the B&M Dive Coaster model (Aethon) has a color scheme matching Griffon's. The trains in the game are also ten across.
